perturbation.original_text: 1.2 TERM. The term of this Agreement will commence on January 1, 1998 (the "Effective Date") and will end on December 31, 1998. Unless either party gives the other at least ninety days' prior written notice that it has elected not to extend the term of this Agreement beyond December 31, 1998, the term of this Agreement will be automatically extended until December 31, 1999. Thereafter this Agreement will automatically be renewed for successive additional periods of one year, unless either party gives notice of cancellation on or before October 1 of any such year.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.2 TERM. The term of this Agreement will commence on January 1, 1998 (the "Effective Date") and will end on December 31, 1998. The term of this agreement can only be extended through a written agreement signed by both parties 30 days prior the end of each year. The Agreement will only continue if both parties agrees to continue the agreement. The agreement cannot be automatically renewed for successive additional periods of one year.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies automatic renewal unless a cancellation notice is given. The modified text now states that the agreement can only be extended through a written agreement signed by both parties, thus contradicting the automatic renewal clause. This contradiction creates uncertainty about how the agreement will extend beyond the initial term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 TERM. The term of this Agreement will commence on January 1, 1998 (the "Effective Date") and will end on December 31, 1998. The term of this agreement can only be extended through a written agreement signed by both parties 30 days prior the end of each year. The Agreement will only continue if both parties agrees to continue the agreement. The agreement cannot be automatically renewed for successive additional periods of one year.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for automatic renewal of the agreement unless notice is given. The changed text explicitly states that the agreement 'cannot be automatically renewed' and requires a written agreement for each extension, creating a direct conflict with the concept of automatic renewal. The contradicted text is the changed text itself, which now contradicts the original intention of automatic renewal. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as it alters the fundamental mechanism for extending the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: SHPS shall provide the Care Management Services directly to the Clients in accordance with the terms of the Client Agreements. "Care Management Services" means the business of providing utilization review (which includes, but is not limited to, pre-admission certification, prior authorization, prospective length of stay approvals, second opinions, concurrent review and discharge planning), catastrophic medical case management, disease management and demand management (24 hours per day, 7 days per week) services to benefits payors and health providers, in all cases in accordance with the terms of the applicable Client Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: SHPS shall provide the Care Management Services directly to the Clients in accordance with the terms of the Client Agreements. "Care Management Services" means the business of providing utilization review (which includes, but is not limited to, pre-admission certification, prior authorization, prospective length of stay approvals, second opinions, concurrent review and discharge planning), catastrophic medical case management, disease management, and demand management services to benefits payors and health providers, in all cases in accordance with the terms of the applicable Client Agreement. These services will be provided during normal business hours, Monday through Friday, excluding holidays.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines "Care Management Services" as 24/7 services. The modified text changes this definition, specifying that services are only provided during normal business hours. This creates a contradiction, as the agreement initially implies round-the-clock service but later restricts it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.1 AGREEMENT TO OUTSOURCE CARE MANAGEMENT SERVICES. HPS agrees to outsource to SHPS, and hereby appoints SHPS as the exclusive provider of, Care Management Services to the Clients, subject to the terms and conditions set forth in this Agreement. SHPS shall provide the Care Management Services directly to the Clients in accordance with the terms of the Client Agreements. "Care Management Services" means the business of providing utilization review (which includes, but is not limited to, pre-admission certification, prior authorization, prospective length of stay approvals, second opinions, concurrent review and discharge planning), catastrophic medical case management, disease management, and demand management services to benefits payors and health providers, in all cases in accordance with the terms of the applicable Client Agreement. These services will be provided during normal business hours, Monday through Friday, excluding holidays.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines Care Management Services as a 24/7 operation. The changed text limits these services to normal business hours. The contradicted text includes both the original definition (implying 24/7 availability) and the modified definition (restricting to business hours), creating a direct and irreconcilable contradiction within the same section of the agreement. This would lead to confusion about the scope of SHPS's obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 2.4 NEW CLIENTS. In the event that HPS (or one of its affiliates other than SHPS) enters into an agreement to provide Care Management Services with a client which is not a Client as of the date of this Agreement (a "New Client"), SHPS shall provide such Care Management Services to the New Client in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. HPS will pay to SHPS all Care Management Revenues collected from such New Client, and SHPS will pay a commission to HPS equal to five percent (5%) of such amount received by SHPS from HPS pursuant to this Section 2.4.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.4 NEW CLIENTS. In the event that HPS (or one of its affiliates other than SHPS) enters into an agreement to provide Care Management Services with a client which is not a Client as of the date of this Agreement (a "New Client"), SHPS shall provide such Care Management Services to the New Client in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. HPS will pay to SHPS all Care Management Revenues collected from such New Client, and SHPS is not required to pay any commission to HPS. SHPS will receive the total amount received by HPS pursuant to this Section 2.4.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specified that SHPS will pay a 5% commission to HPS for new clients. The modified text removes this requirement, stating that SHPS is not required to pay any commission. This creates a direct contradiction regarding the commission structure for new clients.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.4 NEW CLIENTS. In the event that HPS (or one of its affiliates other than SHPS) enters into an agreement to provide Care Management Services with a client which is not a Client as of the date of this Agreement (a "New Client"), SHPS shall provide such Care Management Services to the New Client in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. HPS will pay to SHPS all Care Management Revenues collected from such New Client, and SHPS is not required to pay any commission to HPS. SHPS will receive the total amount received by HPS pursuant to this Section 2.4.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates a 5% commission from SHPS to HPS on revenues from new clients. The changed text explicitly states that SHPS is not required to pay any commission. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict regarding the financial obligations of SHPS, fitting the definition of a structural flaw causing an in-text contradiction, as the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications. The contradicted text, being identical to the changed text, highlights the contradiction because it removes the prior obligation.
